title: Key Words for Fluency
subtitle: Intermediate — Cornell Study Notes
summary: George Woolard | Thomson ELT | 22 Sections · 200+ Key Words · 3,000+ Expressions
focus: B1–B2 Collocation Practice | Focus: Fluency through chunk learning

entries.title: WORLD
content: Verb + world
see the world (travel widely)
change the world
destroy the world
take over the world
Adjective + world
a changing world
an ideal world
the outside world
a safer world
the whole world
World + noun
world affairs
world peace
a world record / world record holder
a world authority (on)
Key phrases
around / throughout the world / the world over (= everywhere)
nothing in the world (= nothing at all)  |  all the time in the world (= plenty of time)
without a care in the world (= not worried)  |  out of this world (= fantastic)
It means the world to me. (= very important)
quick_review: see / change / take over / destroy the world
a changing / ideal / safer world; the outside world
world peace / record / affairs; world authority
Warning: 'set a new world record' → become the world record holder

entries.title: COUNTRY
content: Verb + country
enter a country
flee a country (= escape fast)
paralyse a country
represent a country
run a country
Adjective + country
a foreign / free / neighbouring / underdeveloped / wealthy country
Country (subject) + verb
countries break off (diplomatic) relations
countries go to war
countries gain independence
countries host sporting events
countries sign agreements
Preposition phrases
fight / die for your country  |  deported back to the country they came from
drugs smuggled into the country  |  flee the country
quick_review: flee / enter / paralyse / represent / run a country
foreign / free / neighbouring / underdeveloped / wealthy
break off relations | go to war | gain independence | host events | sign agreements
Warning: 'flee the country' NOT 'flee the nation'

entries.title: HOME
content: Verb + home
get home | head home | leave home | return home | call home | own your home
Home + noun
home address | home cooking | home delivery | home town
Verb + [noun] + home
take a taxi home | see (you) home (= accompany safely) | send (you) home | welcome (you) home
Preposition + home
at home | away from home | from home (work from home) | on the way home
Adjective collocations
an ideal home | a broken home (parents separated) | a holiday home
home-grown vegetables | home-made bread
quick_review: get / head / leave / return / call home
home cooking / delivery / address / town
at home | away from home | on the way home | work from home
broken home = parents separated; home-made = made at home

entries.title: BUILDING
cues: Verbs for what we do to buildings?
content: Verb + building
construct / demolish / convert / evacuate / restore a building
Adjective + building
a derelict building | an empty building | a fine building | a high-rise building
the main building | a public building
Noun + prep + building
appearance of / damage to / entry to / a floor of / the tenants of the building
Describing buildings
attractive / impressive / magnificent / listed building (= protected by law)
quick_review: construct / restore / convert / demolish / evacuate a building
derelict / empty / high-rise / main / public building
damage to | entry to | tenants of | appearance of the building
listed building = cannot be changed without government permission

entries.title: ACCOMMODATION & RENT
content: Verb + accommodation
find / provide / guarantee / live in accommodation
Adjective + accommodation
free / furnished / self-catering / temporary accommodation
Verb + rent
afford / owe / pay / put up / withhold the rent
Key expressions
a shortage/lack of cheap rented accommodation
fall behind with the rent (= unable to pay on time)
rent is due one month in advance
What's the rent on the house?
quick_review: find / provide / guarantee / live in accommodation
free / furnished / self-catering / temporary accommodation
pay / afford / put up / withhold / owe the rent
Warning: fall behind WITH the rent; rent is due IN advance

entries.title: ENVIRONMENT
cues: Verbs for what we do to the environment?
content: Verb + environment
respect / harm / pollute / protect the environment
Adjective + environment (working conditions)
a hostile / male / pleasant / safe / stress-free environment
Noun + prep + environment
damage to / effect on / a threat to / the destruction of the environment
in harmony with the environment
Extra
environmentalist = person who protects the environment
environmentally-friendly products
quick_review: respect / harm / pollute / protect the environment
hostile / pleasant / safe / stress-free working environment
damage to | effect on | threat to | destruction of | in harmony with the environment
environmentally-friendly = good for the environment

entries.title: POLLUTION · FUMES · WASTE · RUBBISH
cues: Verbs with pollution? With fumes? With waste? With rubbish?
content: Verb + pollution
reduce pollution | pollution rises / threatens / destroys
Verb + fumes
fumes pour out | inhale fumes | be overcome by fumes
Verb + waste
create / dump / recycle / dispose of waste
Verb + rubbish
collect / clear up / pick up / recycle rubbish
Key expressions
guidelines for the disposal of hazardous waste
a ban on the dumping of radioactive waste
the stadium was littered with rubbish
quick_review: reduce pollution | fumes pour out / inhale fumes
dump / recycle / dispose of waste
collect / clear up / pick up rubbish
Warning: 'dispose OF waste' not 'dispose waste'

entries.title: EARTHQUAKE · FLOOD · STORM · DAMAGE
content: Verb + earthquake
predict / survive / withstand an earthquake | earthquakes hit / devastate / shake buildings
Flood + verb / Flood + noun
floods cause damage / cut off / hit / sweep away
flood damage / victim / warning / water
Verb + storm
storms break / strike / destroy / rage / die down | be caught in / sleep through a storm
Common storm expressions
badly damaged / blown down / struck by lightning / wrecked / uprooted in a storm
'a storm in a teacup' = a lot of fuss about something unimportant
Verb + damage
cause / insure against / prevent / suffer / repair damage
Adjective + damage
considerable / extensive / minor / minimal / incalculable / serious / superficial damage
irreparable / irreversible / permanent damage
quick_review: earthquakes: hit / devastate / shake; withstand / survive
floods: cause damage / cut off / sweep away; flood warning / victim
storms: rage / die down / strike; struck by lightning / uprooted
damage: cause / prevent / repair; irreparable / extensive / superficial

entries.title: WEATHER · HEAT/TEMPERATURE · AIR · LIGHT · FIRE · WATER · NOISE
content: Weather
Verb: dress for / the weather holds / turns hot/cold / sets in
Adj: boiling hot / bitterly cold / dry / wet / glorious / unpredictable weather
Phrases: a spell of / a change in / the best of the weather; weather permitting
Temperature / Heat
Verb (temp): control / take / rise / fall / reach 40°C
Adj (temp): average / constant / freezing / high temperature
Verb (heat): escape from / feel / generate / lose / withstand heat
Air
Verb: need / pollute / breathe / gasp for / put air into
Adj: fresh / polluted / thin / stale / still / cold air
Phrases: by air | into/through the air | in the open air | vanish into thin air
Light
Verb: block out / need / let in / light fades
Adj: artificial / poor / bright / soft / fading light
Noun+of: a flash / glimmer / source of light
Fire
Verb+fire: start / light / put out / cause / fight / catch fire
Fire+verb: burns / goes out / breaks out / destroys / spreads
Fire+noun: alarm / drill / escape / extinguisher / risk
Water
Verb: boil / drink / pour / spill water; water flows / drips
Adj: fresh / mineral / boiling / drinking water
Noun+of: a drop / shortage / supply of water
Noise & Silence
Verb+noise: make / shut out / can't stand / sleep through noise
Adj+noise: awful / deafening / funny / piercing / squeaking / background noise
Adj+silence: complete / long / stunned / awkward silence
Phrases: drive/sit in silence; observe a minute's silence
quick_review: weather: holds / turns cold / sets in; bitterly cold / glorious
temp: rises / falls / reaches 40°C; room temperature / soar into 40s
air: fresh / thin / stale; vanish into thin air; open-air concert
fire: start vs light (start = deliberate); fire breaks out / spreads
noise: deafening / background / piercing; awkward / stunned silence

entries.title: JOB · CAREER · STAFF & DUTY
content: Verb + job
apply for / get / hold down / look for / lose your job
Adjective + job
challenging / boring / rewarding / responsible / stressful job
full-time / part-time / temporary / permanent job
Noun + prep + job
the perks of / the pressures of / qualifications for the job
job satisfaction | job security
Verb + career
begin / choose / pursue / further / wreck your career
Adjective + career
a brilliant / promising / worthwhile career
Phrases: a change of career | a turning point in | the height/pinnacle of your career
career took off (= became successful) | climb the career ladder
Staff & Duty
Verb+staff: employ / recruit/take on / train / lay off staff
Noun+staff: dedication of / member of / turnover of / vacancies for staff
Verb+duty: carry out / fail in / report for / be suspended from duty
Duty phrases: on/off duty | above and beyond the call of duty | in the line of duty
quick_review: apply for / get / hold down / look for / lose a job
challenging / boring / rewarding / stressful / responsible job
begin / pursue / further / wreck a career; height of your career
employ / train / lay off staff; carry out / report for duty

entries.title: QUALIFICATION · INTERVIEW · SKILL · TRAINING · WAGE/SALARY/PAY
content: Qualification
have / get / study for a qualification; course leads to a qualification
Adj: academic / further / recognised qualification
Interview
have / dread / prepare for / hold an interview; be invited to an interview
make a good impression | don't do yourself justice | come across badly at the interview
Skill
learn / hand down / test / update / equip someone with skills
Adj: basic / communication / computer / practical / social skills
Phrases: it takes great skill | calls for skill | with great skill; a degree of skill
Training
Verb: complete / provide / receive / require training
Adj: basic / formal / intensive / proper / regular training
Noun: training programme / session / course
Wage / Salary / Pay
wage = weekly (manual workers) | salary = monthly (non-manual) | pay = general
earn / live on / demand a higher / tax deducted from your wage/salary
Pay phrases: equal pay | pay rise | pay cut | full pay | sick pay | reject a pay offer
a living wage = enough for basic needs
quick_review: study for / get / recognised / academic qualification
make good impression / do yourself justice / come across well at interview
basic / communication / practical / social skills; learn / update / hand down
wage (weekly, manual) vs salary (monthly, non-manual) vs pay (general)

entries.title: TRIP · HOLIDAY · FLIGHT · DELAY/DESTINATION · PASSENGER · PASSPORT/VISA · LUGGAGE/FARE
content: Trip
Verb: afford / cancel / go on / plan / make / ruin a trip
Adj: free / good / long / round trip | a trip abroad/overseas
Noun+of: the highlight / length / cost / a souvenir of the trip
Warning: trip = go + stay + return; journey = one direction only
Holiday
Verb: book / cancel / enjoy / spend / take / ruin your holiday
Adj: package / public / relaxing / paid / dream holiday
Noun: holiday brochure / resort / weekend / job
Flight
Verb: catch / miss a flight; flights are called / delayed / diverted / cancelled
Adj: bumpy / connecting / direct / long-haul / scheduled / smooth flight
Noun+prep: the cancellation / duration / final call / standby / arrival of the flight
Delay & Destination
Verb: apologise for / avoid / expect / cause / face delays
Adj destination: exotic / intended / new / popular / top destination
Passenger
Passenger+verb: board / proceed to gates / be stranded / be killed
Adj+passenger: angry / drunken / nervous / terrified / business class passenger
Phrases: passengers are required / asked / requested / reminded to...
Passport & Visa
Passport: renew / show / surrender / stamp / steal; passport expires
Adj: forged / new / valid passport
Visa: apply for / extend / need / obtain / overstay; visa expires / is refused
Luggage & Fare
Luggage: pack / insure / lose / weigh / screen / search luggage
Adj fare: exact / flat / full / half / return fare
Fare phrases: fares rise / shoot up / are slashed
quick_review: trip: afford / cancel / ruin; highlight / cost / souvenir of the trip
flight: catch / miss; long-haul / connecting / direct; cancelled / diverted
passenger: board / be stranded; required / requested / reminded to...
passport: valid / forged; renew/show/stamp; visa: apply for / overstay
luggage: pack / screen / insure; fares: rise / shoot up / slashed

entries.title: TRAFFIC · STREET · ROUTE/MAP · ACCIDENT · INJURY
content: Traffic
Verb: direct / divert / hold up / reduce traffic; traffic builds up / thins out
Noun+prep: a build-up / break / noise / queue / volume of traffic
Traffic+noun: congestion / accident / jam / warden
Adj: oncoming / rush-hour / heavy / light traffic
Street
Verb: clean up / patrol / wander through / cross over / parade through the streets
Adj: crowded / deserted / the High Street / one-way / a side street
Phrases: beg in / bump into / stop someone in / come up to you in the street
Route & Map
Verb+route: take / mark / plan; people line a route
Adj+route: alternative / direct / escape / popular / roundabout / scenic route
Verb+map: bring / draw / read / study a map; map shows things
Accident
Verb: avoid / cause / have / reduce / witness; accidents happen
Adj: freak / fatal / minor / serious / tragic accident
Noun+of: the cause / scene / a series of / in the event of an accident
Phrases: an accident waiting to happen; accident black spot
Injury
Verb: prevent / cause / suffer / recover from / be treated for / escape with minor injuries
Noun+of: the pain / reports / risk / a series / full extent of injuries
Phrases: due to / owing to / through / as a result of / following injury
Adj (serious): horrific / appalling / crippling / internal; (minor) = minor
quick_review: traffic: builds up / thins out; heavy / light; traffic jam / congestion
street: patrol / wander through; one-way / deserted; High Street
route: scenic / direct / alternative / escape route
accident: freak / fatal / serious; scene / cause of the accident
injury: suffer / recover from; due to / as a result of injury

entries.title: EDUCATION · COURSE · LESSON · PRACTICE/HOMEWORK · EXAM/MARK
content: Education
Verb: get / give / invest in / return to / pay for your education
Adj: good / higher / nursery / private / religious / secondary / sex education
Noun+prep: access to / aim of / standard of / the right to education
Course
Verb: do / complete / drop out of; schools run courses; course consists of / deals with
Adj: crash / demanding / introductory / on-line / vocational course
Noun+prep: completion of / a place on / entry requirements for a course
Lesson
Verb: have / skip / prepare / begin / give / catch up with lessons
Prep: a lesson about / with / during / in a 40-minute lesson
Warning: lesson = one particular period; class = a group of students
Practice & Homework
Practice: need / have / improve with / come with / learn through practice
Phrases: be out of practice | practice makes perfect | just a question of practice
Homework: give / get / do / correct / hand in / help with homework
Exam & Mark
Verb+exam: revise for / sit / mark / pass / fail / scrape through an exam
Exam phrases: make a mess of / pass with flying colours / a really stiff exam
Verb+mark: get / lose / give / deduct marks
Adj+mark: final / full / good / low / top mark
quick_review: get / invest in / pay for; higher / private / secondary education
do / drop out of / complete a course; crash / vocational / on-line course
revise for / sit / pass / scrape through / fail an exam
get / lose / deduct marks; pass with flying colours; a stiff exam

entries.title: SPORT · TEAM · GAME · RACE/COMPETITION/MATCH · VICTORY · DEFEAT · PRIZE · STRENGTH · ENERGY/EXERCISE
content: Sport
Verb: watch / play / take up / hate sport
Adj: contact / dangerous / indoor / popular / spectator / team sport
Sports+noun: centre / coverage / equipment / events / facilities
Team
Verb: play in/for / support / make / be dropped from a team
Adj: the winning / defeated / strong / evenly-matched team
Team+noun: effort / captain / mates / spirit / sports
Race / Competition / Match
race = faster; competition = for a prize; match = defeat each other
Verb+race: win / withdraw from / take part in / finish / watch / be disqualified
Verb+competition: hold/run / enter / be knocked out of
Verb+match: play a football / tennis / qualifying match
Victory & Defeat
Verb+victory: sweep to / celebrate / lead to / rob of victory
Adj+victory: dramatic / decisive / easy / narrow / surprise / well-earned victory
Verb+defeat: suffer / accept / avoid / avenge a defeat
Adj+defeat: heavy/crushing / humiliating / narrow / shock defeat
Prize
Verb: accept / award / claim / present / share / win a prize
Adj: first / booby / major/top / consolation / fabulous / Nobel prize
Prize+noun: draw / money / winners
Strength & Energy/Exercise
Strength: build up / use / save / underestimate / gather strength
Adj+strength: full / inner / physical / superhuman / superior strength
Energy: have / save / use up / run out of / sap your energy
Noun+of+energy: a great deal of / burst of / source of energy
Adj+exercise: light / regular / strenuous / warm-up exercises
quick_review: sport: play / take up; contact / spectator / team sport
team: make / support / be dropped from; team spirit / captain
race (speed) / competition (prize) / match (defeat)
victory: decisive / narrow; defeat: heavy / humiliating
prize: win / award / claim; build up / save / underestimate strength

entries.title: HEALTH · ILLNESS · DISEASE/INFECTION · STRESS · SMOKING/DRUGS · PAIN · APPOINTMENT/SYMPTOM/TEST · TREATMENT/CURE · OPERATION/DRUG
content: Health
Health+verb: look after / nurse back to / health improves / deteriorates / causes concern
Health+noun: hazard / reasons / risk / warning / the health service
Phrases: bad for / harmful to / essential for / do wonders for your health
Adj: good / excellent / perfect health; bad / poor / fragile / ill health
Illness
Verb: suffer from / cause / diagnose / recover from / treat / have an illness
Adj: chronic / long / serious / sudden illness
Noun+prep: cause / extent / symptom / recurrence / full recovery from an illness
Disease & Infection
Disease: contract / suffer from / prevent / eradicate a disease
Adj+disease: infectious / fatal / incurable / rare disease
Noun+prep: outbreak / spread / cure for / resistance to / risk of disease
Infection: clear up / prevent / suffer from; an infection spreads
Stress
Verb: suffer from / cope with / relieve / thrive on / place stress on
Noun+of: effects / levels / in moments of / a sign / source of stress
Phrases: under a lot of stress | stress-related illnesses | stress management
Smoking & Drugs
Smoking: take up / approve of / cut down on / give up / refrain from smoking
Drugs: take / come off / legalise / seize / supply / smuggle drugs
Drug+noun: abuse / addict / dealer / habit / overdose / trafficking
Pain
Verb: bear / cause / pain passes / gets worse; complain of a pain
Adj: burning / dull / throbbing / sudden / constant / unbearable pain
Verbs to reduce pain: alleviate / ease / deaden / kill / relieve / soothe pain
Appointment, Symptom & Test
Appointment: make / get / keep / miss / cancel / wait for; have an appointment
Symptoms: show / treat / include; symptoms go away / persist
Test: do / repeat / take; have to have some tests; tests positive/negative
Treatment, Cure, Operation, Drug
Treatment: begin / give / undergo / need / respond to treatment
Adj+treatment: effective / new / free / urgent / various treatments
Adj+cure: complete / instant / no known cure
Operation: have / go through with / need / survive / perform an operation
Drug: take / prescribe / inject / test / withdraw; drugs approved/disapproved
Adj+drug: fast-acting / powerful / safe / wonder drug
quick_review: health: look after / nurse back; bad for / do wonders for your health
illness: suffer from / recover from; chronic / sudden / serious illness
stress: cope with / thrive on / relieve; under stress
pain: dull / throbbing / unbearable; ease / kill / soothe pain
treatment: respond to / undergo; cure: complete / instant / no known cure

entries.title: MONEY/CASH · SAVINGS/FORTUNE/DEBT · PRICE · FEE/CHARGE · EXPENSE
content: Money & Cash
Verb+money: lend / borrow / earn / inherit / run out of / save / raise / pay / refund money
Adj+money: extra / missing / spending / pocket money
Verb+cash: pay / accept / carry / have cash on you
Savings, Fortune & Debt
Savings: build up / fall back on / spend / put into; cheat out of savings
Fortune: make / spend / come into / lose / save / cost a fortune
Debt: get into / run up / pay off / be faced with / write off debt
Price
Verb: agree on / afford / haggle over; prices rise/fall
Adj: competitive / extortionate / fixed / full / half price
Adverbs of change: rise sharply / fall dramatically / shoot up / drop / slash prices
Fee & Charge
Fee: charge / increase/raise / pay / waive a fee
Adj+fee: additional / flat / high / record / nominal / normal / reasonable fee
Charge phrases: add a service charge | incur bank charges | waive charges | free of charge
Expense
Verb: be worth / spare no / go to / justify / save the expense
Adj: extra / living / medical / operating / travelling expenses
Phrases: at your own / at great / at no extra expense
quick_review: earn / save / raise / run out of / inherit money
make / spend / lose / come into a fortune; get into / pay off debt
prices rise sharply / fall dramatically / shoot up; extortionate / fixed price
free of charge; waive the fee; nominal / reasonable fee
at your own expense; spare no expense; claim/reimburse expenses

entries.title: FOOD · DRINK · MEAL/DISH · DIET/APPETITE · PARTY
content: Food
Verb: eat / prepare / waste / pick up / serve food; be off your food
Adj: delicious / disgusting / fresh / frozen / raw / hot/spicy food
Noun+of: the consumption / a portion / shortage / variety of food
Types: junk / convenience / fast / organic food
Drink
Verb: have / order / sip / spill / offer a drink
Adj (social): celebratory / farewell / free / quick / relaxing / stiff drink
Adj (non-alc): hot / long / refreshing / soft drinks
Meal & Dish
Verb+meal: make / have / go out for / miss / order / serve / skip a meal
Adj+meal: delicious / heavy / light / hot / proper / ready meal
Adj+dish: expensive / local / rice / vegetarian / national dish
Diet & Appetite
Adj+diet: balanced / healthy / low-fat / poor diet
Verb+diet (losing weight): go on / follow / stick to / watch / cut out of your diet
Verb+appetite: lose / sharpen / work up / spoil your appetite
Party
Verb: arrive at / go to / invite to / plan / throw a party
Adj: all-night / fantastic / noisy / surprise party
Types: dinner / fancy-dress / farewell / house-warming / street party
quick_review: eat / prepare / waste / serve food; junk / organic / convenience food
have / order / sip a drink; stiff drink (strong); refreshing / soft drinks
go out for / skip / order a meal; proper / ready / heavy meal
balanced / healthy / low-fat diet; go on a diet; lose your appetite
throw a party; house-warming / farewell / surprise party

entries.title: FUN/ENTERTAINMENT · JOKE · TELEVISION/PROGRAMME · CONCERT · FAN/AUDIENCE · FILM · MUSIC/SONG
content: Fun & Entertainment
Verb+fun: have / spoil / join in / miss out on / get fun out of
Fun phrases: sounds like fun | full of fun | it's great fun | just a bit of fun
Adj+entertainment: popular / free / in-flight / live / family entertainment
Joke
Verb (story): tell / get / hear / laugh at a joke
Adj: corny / dirty / hilarious / private joke
Verb (trick): play a joke on | can't take a joke | joke fell flat
Television & Programme
TV verbs: switch on/off / turn up/down / watch television; show on TV
TV+noun: coverage / licence / presenter / series
Programme verbs: interrupt / record / broadcast; attract viewers
Adj+programme: favourite / live / violent programme
Concert
Verb: attend / cancel / give / put on / perform in a concert
Adj: brilliant / farewell / live / open-air / badly-organised concert
Noun+prep: a ticket for / live broadcast of / venue for / proceeds from the concert
Fan & Audience
Fan: thank / disappoint the fans; fans pack / besiege venues
Adj+fan: devoted / rival fans; a big fan of
Audience: attract / play to / thrill; audience claps
Adj+audience: captive / family / delighted / invited / live / target audience
Film
Verb: see / watch / shoot / show / release / ban / appear in / star in a film
Adj: action-packed / award-winning / good / low-budget film
Noun+prep: a part in / review of / screening of the film
Music & Song
Verb+music: compose / listen to / perform; music appeals to
Adj+music: background / folk / live / classical / deafening music
Noun+prep: a piece of / beat of / taste in / gift for music
Song types: love / pop / protest / theme song; burst into song
quick_review: have fun / spoil fun / miss out on the fun; just a bit of fun
tell / get / laugh at a joke; corny / hilarious; play a joke on
attend / give / put on a concert; open-air / farewell concert
devoted / rival fans; target / captive / live audience
shoot / release / ban a film; action-packed / low-budget film
background / live / classical music; burst into song

entries.title: LIFE · DEATH · AGE · CHARACTER · CLOTHES/FASHION · APPEARANCE · HABIT/ROUTINE
content: Life
Verb: lose / prolong / risk / save / take your own life
Adj: an easy / new / private / social / everyday life
Noun+of: every aspect / loss / pace / quality / rest of your life; a new lease of life
Death
Verb: cause / escape / hear of / get over / mourn a death
Verb+to death: crushed / bleed / fall / freeze / starve / condemn to death
Adj: early / horrible / violent / sudden / certain death
Noun+of: cause / on the point / in the event of death
Age
Verb: feel / get to my age; guess / lie about / look your age
Adj: average / early / impressionable / mental age; old age; mature for age
Age+noun: range / gap / limit
Character
Verb: reveal / transform / blacken your character
Noun+prep: defamation of / flaw in / stain on / strength of character
Adj (person): forceful / likeable / reformed / shady character
Warning: Use 'personality' not 'character' for: outgoing personality / warm personality
Clothes & Fashion
Verb+clothes: put on / take off / wear / pack / make / fit
Adj: casual / clean / loose / warm clothes
Verb+fashion: be in / keep up with / go out of / come back into fashion
Appearance
Verb: change / improve / neglect / judge by / worry about your appearance
Adj: deceptive / outward / physical / untidy / youthful appearance
Phrases: pride in / change in / similar in / self-conscious about appearance; despite appearances
Habit & Routine
Develop habits: form / pick up a habit
End habits: break / kick / give up / get out of a habit
Adj: anti-social / annoying / disgusting / good habit
Routine: have / stick to / change / get back to / upset your routine
Noun+prep: a break from / change of / matter of routine
quick_review: life: lose / risk / prolong; quality / pace / new lease of life
death: mourn / escape; bleed / starve / freeze to death
habit: form / pick up (develop); break / kick / give up (end)
fashion: in / out of / come back into fashion
appearance: improve / neglect / judge by; youthful / deceptive appearance

entries.title: FRIEND · ENEMY · MARRIAGE · DIVORCE · LOVE · RESPECT · FAMILY
content: Friend
Verb: make / become / remain / meet / visit / bring a friend
Multi-word: lose touch with / approve of / depend on / fall out with your friends
Adj: best / close / mutual / old / friend from work
Phrases: circle of friends | friend of a friend | family and friends | just good friends
Enemy
Verb: make / have / face your enemy
Adj: bitter / dangerous / former / old / sworn enemy
War: attack / kill / defeat / enemy advances/retreats
Marriage
Verb: believe in / rush into / save / disapprove of; marriage breaks down
Adj: arranged / happy / previous / perfect marriage
Prep phrases: related by marriage | sex before marriage
Noun+of: break-up / basis / announcement / offers of marriage
Divorce
Verb: get / go through with / end in / grant a divorce; comes through
Divorce+noun: proceedings / rate / settlement / lawyer
Love
Verb: fall in / feel / need / declare your love; love grows / send your love
Love+noun: affair / child / song / life / letters
Phrases: madly in love | love at first sight | no love lost | true love | undying love
head over heels in love | unrequited love
Respect
Verb: show / gain / have / lose / treat with respect
Adj: greatest / grudging / healthy / mutual respect
Phrases: out of respect for | a mark of respect | with all due respect
Family
Verb: have / support / bring up/raise / start / come from a family
Adj: close / immediate / extended / respectable / single-parent / whole family
Noun+prep: friend of / member of / brains / baby / interests of the family
quick_review: fall out with / lose touch with / depend on / approve of your friends
fall in love / love at first sight / madly in love / undying love
marriage breaks down; rush into / save / believe in marriage
show / gain / lose respect; grudging / mutual / healthy respect
bring up / support / start a family; immediate / extended family

sections.title: SECTION 15: The Body and the Senses

entries.title: HEAD · HAND · HEART · EYE · SIGHT · VIEW · SMELL · TASTE · VOICE · BREATH · SLEEP/DREAM
content: Head
Verb: hit/bang / nod / turn / shake / hang your head; head aches
Adj: bald / big / clear / level head
Idioms: off your head (crazy) | go to your head | went over my head | put heads together
Hand
Verb: raise / wave / wash / hold / join / shake / tie hands
Adj: free / outstretched / steady / bare / capable/safe / dirty hands
Phrases: by hand | good with your hands | get your hands on | keep your hands off
Heart
Verb: heart beats / leaps / sinks; break / transplant a heart
Adj: broken / cold / kind / heavy heart
Phrases: speak from the heart | love with all your heart | learn by heart | from the bottom of your heart | a change of heart
Eye
Verb: protect / strain / make eyes at; can't take eyes off; eyes water
Adj: black / close/watchful / naked / sore eyes
Phrases: gaze into her eyes | bring tears to your eyes | see with my own eyes | up to my eyes (= very busy)
Sight
Verb: come into / disappear from / be hidden from / let out of your sight
Adj: terrifying / beautiful / common / rare / welcome / pathetic sight
Prep: at first sight | by sight | in sight | on sight | out of sight | within sight
View
Verb: admire / block / spoil / come into / be hidden from view
Adj: bird's eye / clear / in full / panoramic / poor / sea view
Describing: breathtaking / spectacular / magnificent / fantastic view
Smell & Taste
Smell verbs: detect / have / get rid of / love; smells fill the room / get worse
Adj+smell: disgusting / revolting / funny / off-putting / delicious / overpowering / stale / unmistakable / distinct
Taste verbs: improve / spoil / take away; leave / lose its taste
Adj+taste: authentic / fresh / metallic / strange / sour/bitter / strong taste
Voice & Breath
Voice: hear / lose / lower / raise / recognise; voice shakes
Adj+voice: muffled / loud / deep / soft / whining voice
Noun+prep: tone of / clarity of voice; at the top of your voice
Breath: catch / hold / take / struggle for breath; breath smells
Prep+breath: on / out of / in one / under your breath
Phrases: take your breath away | a breath of fresh air | with bated breath
Sleep & Dream
Sleep verbs: go to / get to / get some / catch up on / get back to sleep; disturb / send to sleep
Adj+sleep: broken / deep / light / good night's sleep
Dream verbs: have / appear in / wake from a dream
Adj+dream: bad / recurring / vivid dream; sweet dreams
quick_review: head: nod / shake / hang; level head; went over my head
heart: breaks / sinks / leaps; learn by heart; change of heart
sight: at first sight; out of sight; terrifying / welcome sight
smell: overpowering / off-putting / unmistakable; smells fill the room
breath: catch / hold / out of breath; take your breath away

entries.title: FEELING · MOOD · HAPPINESS/PLEASURE · ANGER · FEAR/ANXIETY · WORRY · CONFIDENCE · DISAPPOINTMENT/RELIEF · SURPRISE/SHOCK
content: Feeling
Verb: show / suppress / control / hide / hurt your feelings
Adj: bad / sinking / true / mixed feelings; the feeling is mutual
Feeling+of: apprehension / dissatisfaction / loneliness / guilt / well-being
Mood
Verb: mood changes; depend on / affect / lighten/lift your mood
Adj: bad / good / confident / defiant / festive / lazy mood
Phrases: in the mood for; not in a party mood; he's in one of his moods again
Happiness & Pleasure
Happiness: bring / find / wish you happiness; the key to / pursuit of happiness
Pleasure: give / get / combine with / read for; be a pleasure
Adj+pleasure: endless / great / few / simple / unexpected pleasure
Anger
Verb: arouse / express / control / feel anger; anger subsides
Noun+of: a moment / outburst / surge of anger
Phrases: shake fist / raise voice / stamp foot in anger; eyes blaze with anger
seething with anger | vent your anger | anger festered
Fear & Anxiety
Fear: have / confront / overcome / show; live in fear
Adj+fear: constant / deep / irrational / sudden / worst fear
Anxiety: cause / hide / reduce / increase anxiety
Noun+of+anxiety: effect / levels / sign of anxiety
Worry & Confidence
Worry: cause / discuss / forget / add to your worries
Adj+worry: nagging / unnecessary / constant worry; sick with worry
Confidence: gain / have / lack / lose / boost / destroy your confidence
Noun+of: lack of / blow to / boost to / air of confidence; confidence deserts you
Disappointment, Relief, Surprise & Shock
Disappointment: avoid / express / hide / get over / end in disappointment
Relief: feel / sigh with / come as a relief
Surprise: get / express / spring / take by surprise
Adj+surprise: complete / pleasant / best possible surprise
Shock: get / die of / get over / come as a shock; in a state of shock; be in for a shock
quick_review: mixed feelings / sinking feeling / the feeling is mutual
in the mood for; lift your mood; festive / defiant mood
arouse / control / vent anger; seething with anger
confront / overcome / live in fear; irrational fear
gain / lose / boost / destroy confidence; lack of confidence
spring a surprise; shocked / relieved; sigh with relief

entries.title: GOVERNMENT/ELECTION · VOTE · SOCIETY · JUSTICE · LAW
content: Government & Election
Verb+government: bring down / criticise / elect / form / lead / resign from
Verb+election: boycott / hold / stand for / win/lose / rig the election
Election+noun: a candidate in / gains in / an issue in / a seat in / the turnout in the election
Vote
Verb: have / cast / count / win votes
Adj: casting / popular / single / unanimous vote
Noun+of: majority / thousands of / share / result of votes
Phrases: take a vote | put it to the vote | the vote can go against you
Society
Verb: create / destroy / live in / drop out of / integrate into society
Adj: affluent / civilised / open / industrial / multicultural society
Noun+prep: a cross-section / member / danger to / role in society
Justice & Law
Justice: demand / escape / fight for / uphold / bring to / deny justice
Noun+prep: a fight for / miscarriage of / sense of / travesty of justice
Verb+law: break / change / introduce / enforce / obey the law
Law+verb: applies / prohibits / requires / states
Prep+law: above / against / under / within / by law
Noun+prep+law: a change in / respect for / infringement of / interpretation of the law
quick_review: bring down / form / lead / resign from the government
hold / rig / boycott / stand for / win an election
take a vote; casting / unanimous / popular vote
break / enforce / obey the law; above / against / within the law
fight for / uphold justice; miscarriage / travesty of justice

entries.title: CRIME/CRIMINAL · OFFENCE/OFFENDER/VICTIM · ARREST/CHARGE · EVIDENCE · TRIAL/VERDICT · SENTENCE/FINE · PUNISHMENT/PRISON
content: Crime & Criminal
Verb: fight / cut / solve / commit / be charged with / be convicted of a crime
Adj+crime: petty / serious / terrible / violent crime
Noun+prep: a life of / war on / scene of / victim of a crime
Adj+criminal: common / convicted / hardened / known criminal
Offence, Offender & Victim
Verb+offence: commit / make it an offence / charge with / be convicted of / be fined for
Adj+offence: first / capital / minor / serious offence
Adj+offender: first-time / persistent / sex / worst offender
Adj+victim: easy / innocent / murder victim
Arrest & Charge
Verb+arrest: escape / make / resist; lead to an arrest
Adj+arrest: close / house / wrongful arrest
Verb+charge: deny / face / prove / press charges
Charge phrases: plead guilty to / be cleared of / appear on a charge
Evidence
Verb: have / destroy / gather / give / withhold evidence
Adj: false / circumstantial / overwhelming evidence
Noun+of: a shred of / lack of / in the light of the evidence
Phrases: evidence points to / proves; used in evidence; no evidence that
Trial, Verdict & Sentence/Fine
Trial: await / receive / guarantee / stand / bring to / go on trial
Verdict: reach / return / appeal against a verdict
Sentence: defer / pass / receive / reduce / serve a sentence
Adj+sentence: life / light / lenient / stiff / suspended sentence
Verb+fine: carry / face / get / impose / pay; get away with a fine
Punishment & Prison
Punishment: deserve / escape / receive punishment
Adj+punishment: capital / barbaric / harsh / lenient punishment
Prison: go to / send to / escape from / release from prison
Prison+noun: cell / sentence / conditions / service / population
quick_review: commit / fight / cut / solve crime; petty / violent / serious crime
first / minor / capital / serious offence; first-time / persistent offender
make an arrest; plead guilty / be cleared of a charge
gather / withhold evidence; overwhelming / circumstantial / false
stand trial; reach a verdict; serve / receive a sentence; life / suspended sentence

entries.title: WAR · PEACE · ATTACK · DEFENCE · BOMB/EXPLOSION · CASUALTY · WEAPON/GUN · DISPUTE/STRIKE · CAMPAIGN/DEMONSTRATION
content: War
Verb: be at / declare / prepare for / prevent / win/lose; war breaks out
Adj: civil / conventional / full-scale / futile / nuclear war
Noun+of: the horror / outbreak / prisoner / threat/risk of war
Peace
Verb: bring / make / live in / work for / keep; threaten world peace
Peace+noun: campaign / treaty / process / talks
Noun+prep: commitment to / period of / plea for / symbol of / threat to peace
Attack, Defence, Bomb & Casualty
Verb+attack: carry out / come under / launch / repel / protect against; claim responsibility
Types: knife / bomb / arson / racial / nuclear attack
Defence: break through / strengthen / come to the defence of; defences overwhelmed
Bomb: make / detonate / plant / drop / throw; bombs explode/go off
Casualty: suffer / result in / inflict / reduce casualties
Adj+casualty: civilian / heavy / road casualties
Weapon & Gun
Verb+weapon: ban / develop / supply / lay down weapons
Adj+weapon: hidden / lethal / murder / offensive / superior weapon
Verb+gun: carry / fire / load a gun; gun goes off
Phrasal: pull out / point at / put to / throw down / threaten with a gun
Dispute, Strike, Campaign & Demonstration
Dispute: be involved in / handle / intervene in / settle a dispute
Strike: hold / avert / break / lead to; disrupted by a strike
Campaign: launch / conduct / lead / support a campaign
Adj+campaign: advertising / election / publicity / anti-obesity campaign
Demonstration: hold / take part in / lead to; break up a demonstration
quick_review: war breaks out; civil / nuclear / full-scale war; prisoner of war
peace treaty / process / talks; live in peace; fragile / lasting peace
carry out / launch / repel an attack; claim responsibility
lay down your weapons; lethal / offensive weapon; pull out a gun
settle a dispute; avert / hold a strike; launch a campaign

entries.title: LANGUAGE · CONVERSATION · DISCUSSION · SPEECH · SECRET/RUMOUR/LIE
content: Language
Verb: acquire / master / speak / translate into a language
Adj: bad / foreign / first/native / plain / body language
Expressions: a flair for / good command of / fluent in / proficient in a language
Conversation
Verb: carry on / interrupt / monopolise / overhear / get into conversation
Adj: polite / civilised / private / sensible conversation
Noun+prep: art of / snatches of / topic of / tone of / lull in conversation
Discussion
Verb: have / generate / take part in / widen a discussion
Adj: fruitful / heated / lengthy / detailed / full and frank discussion
Expressions: deep in / under discussion; discussion goes round in circles
Speech
Verb: interrupt / make / prepare / devote to; speech avoids a subject
Adj: controversial / dull / moving / passionate / short / witty / free speech
Secret, Rumour & Lie
Verb+secret: have / keep / tell a secret
Adj: dark / open / closely-guarded / best-kept secret
Verb+rumour: confirm / deny / hear / start; rumours go round
Verb+lie: tell / believe / spread a lie
Warning: a white lie = harmless; blatant / barefaced / outright lie = obvious false
quick_review: acquire / master / speak; fluent in / good command of; body language
carry on / monopolise / get into conversation; art of conversation
heated / fruitful / frank discussion; under discussion
make a moving / witty / passionate speech; free speech
keep a secret; start / deny / confirm a rumour; white lie vs outright lie

entries.title: NEWS · MESSAGE · DOCUMENT · ARTICLE · LETTER · MAIL · ADVERT/PUBLICITY/REPUTATION
content: News
Verb: hear / tell / make / wait for / celebrate / catch up on news; break bad news to

Message
Verb: get / leave / pass on / send / take / give a message
Adj: garbled / good-luck / new / urgent message
Message+of: support / sympathy / thanks / congratulation
Document
Verb: deliver / e-mail / produce / check / shred / sign a document
Computer: create / edit / save / print out / send/receive / scroll through a document
Adj: confidential / forged / important / original / enclosed document
Article, Letter & Mail
Article: read / write / publish; articles contain / appear / criticise / cover a topic
Adj: critical / fascinating / informative / whole article
Letter: write / receive / reply to / open / seal / deliver a letter
Mail: check / send / receive mail; junk mail
quick_review: break bad news to; news travels fast; good/bad/latest news
leave / pass on / take a message; garbled / urgent message
sign / shred / deliver a document; confidential / forged / original
write / publish / read an article; fascinating / informative article

entries.title: MACHINE · EQUIPMENT · COMPUTER/INTERNET · REPAIR
cues: VERB + NOUN: commit a crime, make a mess of, give a speech, break the law
ADJECTIVE + NOUN: severe / fatal / chronic illness; stiff / lenient sentence
NOUN + PREPOSITION: cause of death, victim of crime, threat to peace
NOUN + VERB: war breaks out, disease spreads, floods cause damage
VERB + PREPOSITION: cope WITH stress, fall OUT WITH friends, consist OF
content: Machine & Equipment
Machine: operate / use / maintain a machine; machine breaks down
Equipment: buy / install / use / maintain equipment; state-of-the-art equipment
Adj+equipment: advanced / basic / essential / specialised equipment
Computer & Internet
Computer: use / switch on/off / save to / crash; computer screen / keyboard
Internet: access / browse / surf the internet; go online
Adj: wireless / high-speed connection; download / upload files
Repair
Verb: repair / fix / service / be under repair; need repairing
Phrases: in need of / out of / under repair; beyond repair
WHOLE-BOOK REVIEW & STUDY PLAN
Top 50 Collocations Worth Memorising  [p.]
These high-frequency chunks appear across many sections:
1. make a good impression (at the interview)
2. get into debt / pay off your debts / run up debts
3. fall in love / be madly in love / love at first sight
4. catch / miss a flight; flights are delayed / cancelled
5. pass an exam with flying colours / scrape through / revise for
6. take up / give up / cut down on smoking
7. cause / prevent / repair / suffer damage
8. break the law / enforce the law / within the law
9. launch / support / conduct a campaign
10. commit / fight / solve / cut crime
11. suffer from / recover from / diagnose an illness
12. cope with / relieve / thrive on stress
13. gain / lose / boost your confidence
14. hold / avert / break a strike
15. sign / break off / go to war (countries + verbs)
16. save / earn / run out of / raise money
17. build up / fall back on / spend your savings
18. book / cancel / enjoy a holiday
19. carry out / launch / repel an attack
20. make / hold / attend a concert / meeting
21. come into / disappear from / be hidden from sight
22. give / get / take out / attend a lesson / course
23. go on / stick to / follow a diet
24. work up / lose / spoil your appetite
25. take your breath away / catch your breath / out of breath
26. shatter / boost / destroy your confidence
27. reach / appeal against a verdict
28. bring / make / work for peace
29. bring good / bad news; break bad news to
30. a feeling of guilt / loneliness / well-being
31. have / give / attend / cancel / miss an appointment
32. respond to / undergo / begin treatment
33. provide / receive / complete / require training
34. gather / withhold / destroy evidence
35. keep / tell / have a secret; a closely-guarded secret
36. carry on / get into / monopolise a conversation
37. take / cast / count / win votes
38. protect / harm / respect / pollute the environment
39. pour out / inhale / be overcome by fumes
40. sweep to / celebrate / lead to victory
41. suffer / accept / avenge a defeat
42. press / face / deny a charge
43. arouse / control / express anger; seething with anger
44. show / gain / mutual respect
45. drop out of / integrate into society
46. go to / bring to / stand trial
47. apply for / extend / overstay a visa
48. pack / insure / screen / lose luggage
49. do wonders for / essential for good health
50. buy / spare no / justify the expense
Key Collocation Patterns  [p.]
Fluency Sentence Chunks — Learn as Whole Phrases  [p.]
Spaced Repetition Study Plan  [p.]
☐ Day 1: Read Sections 1–3 (Place, Environment, Natural World) + Quick Review each unit
☐ Day 3: Review Sections 1–3 using CUE column only. Add Sections 4–6 (Work, Travel, Traffic).
☐ Day 7: Review all 6 sections. Add Sections 7–10 (Education, Sport, Health, Money).
☐ Day 14: Full sweep of all 10. Add Sections 11–15 (Food, Entertainment, People, Relationships, Body).
☐ Day 21: Review 1–15. Add Sections 16–22 (Feelings, Society, Crime, Conflict, Comms, Info, Tech).
☐ Day 30: Full book review — Cue column only. Write 3 sentences per section from memory.
☐ Ongoing: Pick 5 chunks per day from Top 50 list. Use them in writing or speech that day.
MASTER QUICK REFERENCE — All 22 Sections
Topic

S1
Place
see/change the world; flee a country; home cooking; construct/demolish building; afford the rent

Environment

S3
Natural
bitterly cold weather; fresh air; fire breaks out/spreads; stale/overpowering smell; stunned silence

Work

S5
Travel
catch/miss a flight; book a holiday; overstay a visa; screen luggage; fares slashed

Traffic

S7
Education

S8
Sport

S9
Health
nurse back to health; chronic illness; cope with stress; soothe pain; undergo treatment
Money

Food

Fun

People

Relations

Body

Feeling

Society

Crime

Conflict

It means the world to me.
We've got all the time in the world.
The food was out of this world!
She had no love lost between them.
The accident was an accident waiting to happen.
It was a storm in a teacup.
Practice makes perfect.
I'm dying for a drink.
He's in one of his moods again.
She's up to her eyes in work.
Don't take your anger out on me.
Their marriage is on the rocks.
I don't see eye to eye with my father.
It's not in his character to do that.
The news came as a complete surprise.
I'm afraid there's no known cure.
Considering the amount of stress she's under...
We've been sick with worry!
The verdict came as a great relief.
I got the shock of my life.
I had a sinking feeling in the pit of my stomach.
She passed the exam with flying colours.
